name: Electronics for control units; at plant
description:
This cradle-to-gate unit process dataset is for the production of 1 kg  of  electronics for control units, which are equated to a solar optimizer according to Kneifel et al. (2022).
This unit process includes raw material acquisition; manufacturing;  manufacture and installation of capital equipment; and transport.
category: 31-33: Manufacturing/3345: Navigational, Measuring, Electromedical, and Control Instruments Manufacturing

processDocumentation.timeDescription: Althaus et al. (2007) does not specify exact dates, but the Start and the end date are for the calendar years 1990 and 2005.
processDocumentation.geographyDescription: Althaus et al (2009) indicated data had no specific geographical origin, as values came from literature.
processDocumentation.technologyDescription:
This process represents the production of solar optimizers using an estimated composition of the electronics for control units in the chemical industry based on literature from the calendar years 1990-2004.
Control units are used in the chemical industry to operate each device. The main components of such a control unit are (i) the electronic parts (usually some mounted PWB), (ii) cables (internal, but also for relying the control unit to the device) and, (iii) the housing around the whole unit (Althaus et al., 2007).
processDocumentation.modelingConstantsDescription: Althaus et al. (2007) assumed a standard distance of 100 km for truck transport, and 200 km for train transport.
processDocumentation.completenessDescription:
Treatment of Missing Environmental Data
Althaus et al. (2007) offered no information on cutoff criteria or flows that might have not been considered.

Treatment of Missing Technosphere Document
Althaus et al. (2007) offered no information on cutoff criteria or flows that might have not been considered.

Mass Balance
No mass balance was calculated for this process.
processDocumentation.dataSelectionDescription: This unit process was modeled based on Althaus et al. (2007) inventory for electronics for control units available on Table 2.8 of said report.
processDocumentation.dataTreatmentDescription:
The values in the LCI in Althaus et al. (2007) reflect the expert judgement of the authors, since they do not seem a weighted average of the different studies.

Process data quality
Based on the information included in the “Data completeness section”, the data quality characteristic (DQC) ""Process completeness"" was given a score of 2 (60-79 % of determined flow have been evaluated and given a value). It is likely the original report went through a review process, but because it is not explicitly mentioned, the data quality characteristic ""Process review"" was left as default (No documented review) and thus with a score of 5."
processDocumentation.samplingDescription:
System boundaries
The system boundary is cradle to gate and includes material, transport, and infrastructure. 

Data collection
The data that made Althaus et al. (2007)) LCI is based on own estimations and values from Frischknecht et al. (1996). See the original source for details.

Uncertainty
Althaus et al. (2007) included a standard deviation for all flows, which is repeated here.
processDocumentation.dataCollectionDescription: Between calendar years 1990 and 2004, both included, based Althaus et al. (2007)

sources.name: Frischknecht et al. (1996) LCA Energy Systems

reviews.details: Unlike other sections in Althaus et al. (2009), the one on Electronics for control units does not mention any reviewer.
Unlike other sections in Althaus et al. (2009), the one on Electronics for control units does not mention any reviewer.

processDocumentation.intendedApplication:
Situation C2 - Accounting, without system-external interactions - The intended application is a purely descriptive accounting / documentation of the analyzed system excluding interactions with other systems in the LCI model.
The target audience of this model are LCA practitioners, the silicon, solar and related industries and the general public. The inventory is considered largely complete, except for land transformation activities. For that reason, it is not recommended to use this dataset when evaluating land use impact categories. The results of the National Institute of Standards and Technology’s data gap analysis were analyzed using the ISO 21930-LCIA-US impact assessment methodology, as well as Cumulative Energy Demand and a subset of ReCiPe 2016 impact categories.
The original application of this dataset was the identification of data gaps in the Federal LCA Commons (FLCAC) (see ""Project"" above). However, the intended application is to provide a cradle-to-gate inventory (A1-A3, according to ISO 21930) using exclusively processes currently available in USLCI or generated as part of the project mentioned above, and elementary flows from the Federal Elementary Flow List (FEDEFL) to enable the use of a fuller range of LCIA impact categories than those reported in EPDs. For data quality purposes, the intended application is to provide a US average inventory for the year of publication of the original source.
processDocumentation.projectDescription: This dataset was developed as part of the “Public Life Cycle Inventory Data Gap Analysis for Silicon-based Residential Photovoltaic Systems” National Institute of Standards and Technology’s Technical Note.
